metadata.engine_name: OTC Derivatives Trade Reporting Compliance Engine

EventCategory.Description: Category of the reference event that determines settlement

ReferenceEntity.Description: Body or index whose decision or outcome defines the event
ReferenceEntity.Example: German Federal Election Commission | US Bureau of Labor Statistics | ESMA

EventJurisdiction.Description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the primary jurisdiction

EventDate.Description: Expected event determination date in ISO 8601 format

ResolutionSource.Description: Official authoritative source used to determine final settlement outcome
ResolutionSource.Example: Official federal election results | BLS CPI release | ESMA register

SettlementCurrency.Description: Settlement currency: ISO 4217 fiat or stablecoin identifier
SettlementCurrency.Note: Stablecoin codes such as USDC are not ISO 4217 and require a supplemental identifier scheme.

PlatformType.Description: Legal and operational classification of the trading venue

RegulatoryStatus.Description: Regulatory classification of the contract in the applicable jurisdiction

quantifiable_exposure.rationale: A renewable-energy firm with German government subsidy exposure can identify revenue sensitivity to the election outcome.

viable_hedge_substitute.rationale: Power forwards, carbon allowances, FX/rates hedges, or structured derivatives can hedge some cash-flow channels, but not the binary political trigger directly.

price_discovery_beyond_public_data.rationale: Regulated prediction-market prices can provide a continuously updated probability signal beyond static polling averages.

quantifiable_exposure.rationale: A fixed-income portfolio manager holding USD rates exposure can quantify CPI-driven mark-to-market losses.

viable_hedge_substitute.rationale: The payoff is economically analogous to CPI caps or inflation swaptions, but the offshore venue, missing LEIs, missing UTI, and USDC settlement block ordinary CFTC/EMIR reporting visibility.

price_discovery_beyond_public_data.rationale: Binary CPI prices can provide distributional probability information not directly observable from ordinary fixed-income instruments.

quantifiable_exposure.rationale: A fintech firm can quantify the compliance-cost timing effect of an AI Act deadline extension.

viable_hedge_substitute.rationale: No standard OTC instrument references the ESMA AI Act deadline as the underlying event.

price_discovery_beyond_public_data.rationale: The event may contain a useful regulatory probability signal, but thin volume and possible non-public information weaken informational reliability.

regulatory_references.name: CFTC Prediction Markets ANPR
regulatory_references.relevance: Confirms the CFTC is seeking comment on event-contract derivatives traded on prediction markets and can use comments to inform future rulemaking.

regulatory_references.name: Diercks, Katz and Wright (2026)
regulatory_references.citation: Kalshi and the Rise of Macro Markets, NBER Working Paper 34702 / FEDS 2026-010
regulatory_references.relevance: Supports the price-discovery premise for regulated macro prediction markets.

regulatory_references.name: Brandes (2026)
regulatory_references.citation: The Unhedgeable State: Why Europe's Risk Management Architecture Has a Political Risk Gap
regulatory_references.relevance: Supports functional classification based on operator neutrality, financial exposure, and incremental price discovery.

cftc_recommendations.title: Extend SDR-style reporting to CFTC-licensed DCM event contracts above a materiality threshold.
cftc_recommendations.engine_impact: T026 and T028 would move from CFTC_EVENT_CONDITIONAL to a directly reportable event-contract scope if the rule supplied UPI, LEI, UTI, event, and venue fields.

cftc_recommendations.title: Create a novel-instrument notification or review trigger for offshore or stablecoin-settled event contracts.
cftc_recommendations.engine_impact: T027 combines non-ISO USDC settlement, missing LEIs, missing UTI, no UPI taxonomy, and offshore/decentralised venue facts. Its notional is below the illustrative USD 100,000 filing threshold, so the engine treats it as a review flag rather than an automatic filing obligation.

source_facts.event_description: AfD > 30% in Bundestagswahl German federal election

required_attribute_validation.note: No ANNA-DSB OTC UPI template exists for EventContract, so template-level attribute validation cannot be performed.

notes: EventContract has no ANNA-DSB UPI product definition in the OTC taxonomy.

quantifiable_exposure.rationale: A renewable-energy firm with German government subsidy exposure can identify revenue sensitivity to the election outcome.

viable_hedge_substitute.rationale: Power forwards, carbon allowances, FX/rates hedges, or structured derivatives can hedge some cash-flow channels, but not the binary political trigger directly.

price_discovery_beyond_public_data.rationale: Regulated prediction-market prices can provide a continuously updated probability signal beyond static polling averages.

compliance_findings.message: No ANNA-DSB OTC product definition exists for EventContract; this is a taxonomy gap, not a parser crash.

scope_assessments.message: T026 is a Kalshi-style event contract on a CFTC DCM. Treat as CONDITIONAL rather than a conventional OTC swap.
Case-file facts mark this EventContract as outside the selected EMIR OTC reporting path.
